---
title: "Dive Data Exploration"
author: "Kierra Miller"
date: "2026-06-01"
categories:
  - Data Analysis
  - Scuba Diving
format: html
---

# Dive Depth Data Visualization 

```{r}
# create data set of dive depths 
depths <- c(
38,40,44,15,40,40,
50,40,44,35,32,32,60
)

summary(depths)

# create visualization 
library(ggplot2)

dives <- data.frame(
  Dive = 1:length(depths),
  Depth = depths
)
# plot x and y axes, set labels and titles
ggplot(dives,
       aes(x = Dive,
           y = Depth)) +
  geom_col() +
  labs(
    title = "Depth of Recorded Dives",
    x = "Dive Number",
    y = "Depth (ft)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

```

# Results

The average dive depth was approximately 39 feet. Most dives ranged between 32 and 50 feet, while the deepest dive reached 60 feet.

This exercise demonstrates how ecological and recreational field data can be explored using simple visualization techniques in R.
